AssetVersionCreate.description:
Create a version. Either PROMOTE from an existing artifact (`from_artifact_id`,
zero-copy: the version copies the artifact's content_hash + full r2:// uri + size)
OR supply the pointer identity directly.
AssetVersionCreate.title: AssetVersionCreate

ClientRegistrationIn.description:
RFC 7591 dynamic client registration request. We accept the standard
metadata but fix grant/response types and the (none) auth method ourselves.
ClientRegistrationIn.title: ClientRegistrationIn

DeviceAuthorizationStart.description:
Start a first-party CLI browser handoff.

PKCE is mandatory even though the high-entropy device code is already a
bearer secret: it binds the exchange to the CLI process that initiated it
if a terminal transcript or launch URL is copied elsewhere.
DeviceAuthorizationStart.title: DeviceAuthorizationStart

ExperimentPatch.description:
Field-replace PATCH (D9). Only provided fields change; None = untouched.
`hypothesis`/`name` carry min_length=1 so a PATCH can never re-blank them.
ExperimentPatch.title: ExperimentPatch

ExperimentVersionMint.description:
Mint an immutable reproduction manifest (fold #6). `as_of` defaults to the
mint-time watermark; `exclude_run_ids` are recorded and left out of the refs.
ExperimentVersionMint.title: ExperimentVersionMint

LineageEntityType.description: The endpoints a lineage edge can connect (closed set).
LineageEntityType.title: LineageEntityType

MetricSeriesOut.description: One catalog row: what/how-much/what-shape, no point scan (D15).
MetricSeriesOut.title: MetricSeriesOut

PendingInviteOut.description: An invite shown to the invitee on /onboarding (joined to team name).
PendingInviteOut.title: PendingInviteOut

ProjectArtifactCreate.description: Project-anchored artifact (fold #22); same shape as the experiment-anchored one.
ProjectArtifactCreate.title: ProjectArtifactCreate

ProjectPatch.description: Field-replace PATCH (D9). Only provided fields change; None = untouched.
ProjectPatch.title: ProjectPatch

RunBundle.description:
One request: run + catalog + artifact index (bounded) + spans overview +
lineage refs. Raw points are excluded (fetch via /series/query).
RunBundle.title: RunBundle

RunCounts.description: Child-row tallies nested under RunOut.counts (dashboard contract).
RunCounts.title: RunCounts

RunDetailOut.description:
The /v1 read view of a run: RunOut plus the fold fields that are DELIBERATELY
absent from RunOut so the frozen ingest response stays byte-identical (the ingest
route keeps response_model=RunOut, which filters these out). short_id (fold #21)
is the human-facing petname the dashboard displays; created_by (fold #1), env_ref
(fold #7), and foreign_keys (fold #8) surface here for reads.
RunDetailOut.title: RunDetailOut

RunGcRequest.description:
Permanently purge deleted runs (owner/admin). Exactly one selector: an
explicit id list, or every run soft-deleted before `older_than`.
RunGcRequest.title: RunGcRequest

RunGroupPatch.description: Field-replace PATCH (D9). Only provided fields change; None = untouched.
RunGroupPatch.title: RunGroupPatch

RunLineage.description: Ancestors + descendants of a run (recursive-CTE result).
RunLineage.title: RunLineage

Scope.description:
Coarse v1 authorization scopes (D2). Narrowable later; checked as explicit
sets per route, NOT as a hierarchy (a `write` token does not imply `read`).
Scope.title: Scope

ScopedUploadRequest.description:
Upload fields shared by project and experiment anchors.

The anchor comes exclusively from the URL and the R2 pointer/key are always
server-built. Forbid extras so a client-supplied ``uri`` or ``key`` is rejected
instead of being silently ignored by Pydantic.
ScopedUploadRequest.title: ScopedUploadRequest

SeriesQueryRequest.description: Multi-run, multi-series read for the run page AND N-run comparison (D9).
SeriesQueryRequest.title: SeriesQueryRequest

SpanUpsertResult.description: Typed replacement for the bare `{"upserted": n}` dict (D9).
SpanUpsertResult.title: SpanUpsertResult

TeamCreate.description:
POST /auth/teams. `slug` format/uniqueness is validated
server-side (app.teams.service) so the 400/409 carries a helpful message +
a collision suggestion; the model only bounds length.
TeamCreate.title: TeamCreate

TeamMemberOut.description: One active team membership joined to its human identity.
TeamMemberOut.title: TeamMemberOut

TeamMemberRoleUpdate.title: TeamMemberRoleUpdate

TokenCreate.description:
POST /v1/tokens (session-only mint). `scopes=None` inherits the caller's
role; e.g. `['read']` mints a read-only 'notebook' token. `expires_at=None`
means non-expiring (house precedent).
TokenCreate.title: TokenCreate

TokenCreated.description:
Mint response — carries the plaintext secret ONCE (never stored, never
shown again).
TokenCreated.title: TokenCreated

TokenOut.description: A token as listed (never carries the secret).
TokenOut.title: TokenOut

get.description: Verify the ID token, upsert the user, mint a session, 302 to dashboard.

post.description: Create a short-lived request. No user/session exists at this stage.

get.description: Consent metadata for the signed-in browser; never returns a secret.

post.description: Explicit session-only Allow/Deny with an atomic pending-state CAS.
Poll/exchange a decided request; mint at most one user-bound PAT.

get.description: Invites addressed to the signed-in user's verified email (onboarding).

schema.title: Response Pending Invites Auth Invites Pending Get

get.description: Begin the OIDC flow: stash the (validated) redirect, 302 to Google.

post.description: Revoke the session and clear the cookie (idempotent).

get.description: The signed-in user + their teams + the active team.

post.description:
Set the session's active team -- ONLY if the user is a member of it.

The body's customer_id is never trusted without the membership check; an
unowned team yields 403.

schema.title: Response Switch Team Auth Switch Team Post
Response List Invites Auth Teams Invites Get
Response List Members Auth Teams Members Get

delete.description: Remove a non-owner member and immediately clear their active team sessions.

patch.description: Promote/demote a non-owner member. Ownership transfer is not a v1 flow.

post.description:
Purge abandoned pending uploads older than `older_than` (owner/admin). DB rows
only -- R2 blob GC stays OFF (a pending row has no confirmed object).

delete.description:
Soft-delete the artifact row (idempotent-safe: an already-deleted or absent
artifact -> 404). The R2 blob is intentionally left in place (deferred gc).

schema.title: Response List Assets V1 Assets Get
Response List Versions V1 Assets  Asset Id  Versions Get
Response List Events V1 Events Get
Response List Experiments V1 Experiments Get
Response List Experiment Artifacts V1 Experiments  Experiment Id  Artifacts Get

get.description: Every edge touching a run or artifact that belongs to this experiment.

schema.title: Response List Experiment Edges V1 Experiments  Experiment Id  Edges Get
Response List Groups V1 Experiments  Experiment Id  Groups Get
Response List Versions V1 Experiments  Experiment Id  Versions Get

get.description:
The authenticated caller: identity + resolved tenant, role, and scopes.

`require_scopes()` with no required scope accepts any authenticated principal,
so this works for a session cookie, a `ros_pat`, or an OAuth access token.

schema.title: Response List Projects V1 Projects Get
Response List Project Artifacts V1 Projects  Project Id  Artifacts Get
Response List Runs V1 Runs Get

post.description:
Permanently purge soft-deleted runs (owner/admin). Selects by explicit id
list OR everything deleted before `older_than` (exactly one). Purges DB ROWS
ONLY — spans/metrics/artifacts CASCADE; R2 blobs are NOT touched (deferred).
Each purged run's children have their parent_run_id + parent_relation cleared
together first, so no dangling lineage marker is left behind.

delete.description:
Soft-delete: hide the run everywhere until restore or gc. The natural key
stays reserved. Deleting an already-deleted (or absent) run -> 404.

get.description:
List a run's artifacts, optionally filtered by kind and/or step range.

``kind`` matches the normalized (lowercased) column. Step bounds are
inclusive and compare against ``step_index``, so rows with no step are
excluded whenever either bound is set (SQL NULL comparison) — exactly what
step-scoped forensics ("sandbox state at steps 599..601") wants.

schema.title: Response List Artifacts V1 Runs  Run Id  Artifacts Get
Response List Run Edges V1 Runs  Run Id  Edges Get
Response List Run Events V1 Runs  Run Id  Events Get
Response List Metrics V1 Runs  Run Id  Metrics Get

post.description:
Un-delete a soft-deleted run. 404 if the run does not exist in-tenant;
a no-op (still 200) if it was already live.

schema.title: Response Get Series Catalog V1 Runs  Run Id  Series Get
Response List Spans V1 Runs  Run Id  Spans Get

post.description: Structure-only sugar: mints a step span. Numbers go through /metrics.

get.description: `run_ref` is a UUID or a petname short_id (fold #21); resolved unambiguously.

The run-page JSON dump (D9): run + catalog + bounded artifact index +
span-type overview + lineage refs. Raw points come from /series/query.
`run_ref` is a UUID or a petname short_id. 404 on a soft-deleted run;
child_run_ids + artifacts exclude deleted rows.

post.description:
One request serves the run page (1 run) and the comparison view (N runs).
RLS scopes the read; every run_id is validated in-tenant AND live (a soft-
deleted run is hidden -> 404) before any points are read.

get.description: Flat single-GET (D9). Hidden when its run is soft-deleted.

schema.title: Response List Tokens V1 Tokens Get

delete.description:
CLI logout: the calling token revokes itself. 400 if the caller is a
session (there is no 'current token').

Revoke a token by id. Your own token: any writer. A teammate's token:
owner/admin (`admin` scope) AND a browser session (never a token).
